title: Continual Generalized Intent Discovery: Marching Towards Dynamic and Open-world Intent Recognition
authors: Xiaoshuai Song; Yutao Mou; Keqing He; Yueyan Qiu; Pei Wang; Weiran Xu
key issue: Existing GID pipelines rely on joint training over historical OOD stages; the core challenge is continual open-world intent discovery with little access to past data.
future look: Open-world unknown intent/risk discovery and deployment in dynamic streams

title: IntentGPT: Few-shot Intent Discovery with Large Language Models
authors: Juan A. Rodriguez; Nicholas Botzer; David Vazquez; Christopher Pal; Marco Pedersoli; Issam Laradji
key issue: Most intent discovery approaches need substantial labeled data or fine-tuning; the key challenge is discovering emerging intents in few-shot and training-light settings.
backbone/framework: In-context prompt generator + semantic few-shot sampler
future look: Few-shot/open-world intent discovery with lower annotation cost

title: LLM Performance Predictors: Learning When to Escalate in Hybrid Human-AI Moderation Systems
authors: Or Bachar; Or Levi; Sardhendu Mishra; Adi Levi; Manpreet Singh Minhas; Justin Miller; Omer Ben-Porat; Eilon Sheetrit; Jonathan Morra
key issue: In hybrid moderation, the central challenge is uncertainty-aware escalation: deciding when to trust model outputs and when to defer to humans under explicit accuracy-cost trade-offs.
future look: Cost-aware human-AI moderation and uncertainty calibration

title: Learning to Defer in Content Moderation: The Human-AI Interplay
key issue: Classical defer assumes fixed review cost and immediate feedback; the key challenge is jointly optimizing classify/admit/schedule decisions under queue congestion and delayed feedback.
backbone/framework: Learning-to-defer with contextual queueing and delayed feedback
future look: Defer policies with congestion-aware human review allocation

title: Toxicity Detection towards Adaptability to Changing Perturbations
authors: Hankun Kang; Jianhao Chen; Yongqi Li; Xin Miao; Mayi Xu; Ming Zhong; Yuanyuan Zhu; Tieyun Qian
key issue: Toxicity detectors are fragile to newly emerging jailbreak perturbations; the key challenge is domain-incremental robustness to continually evolving attack patterns.
backbone/framework: Domain-incremental toxicity detector under evolving perturbations
dataset: New perturbation-aware toxicity dataset (9 jailbreak/perturbation patterns)
future look: Continual robustness against emerging jailbreak/perturbation patterns

title: GeRe: Towards Efficient Anti-Forgetting in Continual Learning of LLM via General Samples Replay
authors: Yunan Zhang; Shuoran Jiang; Mengchen Zhao; Yuefeng Li; Yang Fan; Xiangping Wu; Qingcai Chen
key issue: Continual LLM fine-tuning suffers dual forgetting (general capability + past tasks); the key challenge is efficient replay that preserves both with low overhead.
backbone/framework: General sample replay + threshold-margin state consistency
dataset: Sequential multi-domain LLM continual learning tasks
evaluation metrics: General capability retention; previous-task performance; forgetting
future look: Efficient anti-forgetting continual tuning for LLM safety adaptation

title: Lifelong Pretraining: Continually Adapting Language Models to Emerging Corpora
authors: Xisen Jin; Dejiao Zhang; Henghui Zhu; Wei Xiao; Shang-Wen Li; Xiaokai Wei; Andrew Arnold; Xiang Ren
key issue: PTLMs pretrained once on static corpora degrade under domain/time drift; the key challenge is continual pretraining that adapts to emerging corpora without catastrophic forgetting.
backbone/framework: Incremental PTLM pretraining with distillation-based retention
dataset: Domain-incremental research-paper stream; chronological tweet stream
evaluation metrics: Downstream performance over time; retention and transfer under temporal drift
future look: Lifelong adaptation of language models to evolving corpora
